question_groups.instruction: Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

items.prompt_html: The amount of research on giraffes in their natural environment has increased lately.
Megan Strauss disapproves of keeping giraffes in zoos.
The number of giraffes in Africa has nearly doubled.
Female giraffes tend to live longer than males.
Mother giraffes sometimes use their legs to defend their young.
Younger bulls hold their necks higher in the presence of more powerful males.
Male giraffes have been observed fighting.

options.label: TRUE
options.text: if the statement agrees with the information

options.label: FALSE
options.text: if the statement contradicts the information

options.label: NOT GIVEN
options.text: if there is no information on this

question_groups.instruction:
Look at the following statements and the list of researchers below.
Match each statement with the correct researcher, A, B, C, or D.
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C, or D , in boxes 20-23 .
NB You may use any letter more than once.

List of Researchers
A Joseph Allen
B Craig Zimring
C Piers MacNaughton
D Amy Eyler

items.prompt_html: Further investigations will result in improved wellbeing and efficiency.
A person's opinion of their workspace may influence how well they perform at work.
The design of a workplace can have an impact on how much staff work with their colleagues.
A worker's mental capacity may be considerably reduced due to their working environment.

Harvard study published in Environmental Health Perspectives

Allen's first study was done at the Syracuse Center, which focuses on researching <blank data-qid="q24"/>. In this study, a complex ventilation system was used to control the airflow in the simulated workplace. All staff carried out normal duties during the day without much . Later in the day participants were assessed as they <blank data-qid="q25"/> performed tasks which required them to play a role, for example acting as <blank data-qid="q26"/> while dealing with practical problems.

question_groups.instruction:
Reading Passage 3 has eight paragraphs, A–H .
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Choose the correct letter, A–H , in boxes 27–31 .

items.prompt_html: economic evidence for play in medieval England
an example of how children created their own toys
an example of warlike play approved by grown-ups
a political event mirrored in children’s play
the origin of an academic debate about medieval life

Choose YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer, choose NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer, or NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

items.prompt_html: Little is known about adult attitudes to children’s play in the Middle Ages.
Most toys were imported into England before 1300.
Medieval children celebrated the seasons in their own ways.
Medieval children made presents for their parents on special occasions.
Parents and children in the Middle Ages behaved much as they do today.

options.label: YES
options.text: if the statement agrees with the writer's claims or views

options.label: NO
options.text: if the statement contradicts the writer's claims or views

options.label: NOT GIVEN
options.text: if it is impossible to say from the passage
